name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 29.09.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 29.09.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Sowing Status: As on 26 September, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1120.73 lakh ha as compared to 1113.72 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 102.2 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.
texts.text: Kharif Sowing Status: As on 26 September, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1120.73 lakh ha as compared to 1113.72 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 102.2 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal in NW India, Bihar, NE India and Gujarat.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal in NW India, Bihar, NE India and Gujarat.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall Distribution: From June 01 -Sept 28 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 8% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)20%; (ii) Northwest India: 28%; (iii) Central India: 14%; and South Peninsula: 11%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From June 01 -Sept 28 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 8% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)20%; (ii) Northwest India: 28%; (iii) Central India: 14%; and South Peninsula: 11%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Heavy to very heavy rainfall likely at some places over Konkan, Madhya Maharashtra, Gujarat  state  during  28 th   September  -  01 st   October.  Isolated  extremely  heavy  rainfall  is also likely over north Konkan (including Mumbai), north Madhya Maharashtra and south Gujarat Region on 28th; Saurashtra & Kutch on 29 th  September.
texts.text: Heavy to very heavy rainfall likely at some places over Konkan, Madhya Maharashtra, Gujarat  state  during  28 th   September  -  01 st   October.  Isolated  extremely  heavy  rainfall  is also likely over north Konkan (including Mumbai), north Madhya Maharashtra and south Gujarat Region on 28th; Saurashtra & Kutch on 29 th  September.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Thunderstorm with lightning very likely over West Rajasthan on 29 th  & 30 th  September and East Rajasthan during next 5 days.
texts.text: Thunderstorm with lightning very likely over West Rajasthan on 29 th  & 30 th  September and East Rajasthan during next 5 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Isolated to scattered rainfall activity likely over many parts of the country except Gangetic Plains,  East  Rajasthan,  Madhya Pradesh, West & Central India where fairy widespread rainfall activity is likely.
texts.text: Isolated to scattered rainfall activity likely over many parts of the country except Gangetic Plains,  East  Rajasthan,  Madhya Pradesh, West & Central India where fairy widespread rainfall activity is likely.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Rainfall is likely to be normal to above normal over most parts of West India, many parts of northwest and adjoining western Himalayan region & central India and along the West Coast. Below normal rainfall is likely in many parts of Northeast & adjoining East India and South Peninsular India.
texts.text: Rainfall is likely to be normal to above normal over most parts of West India, many parts of northwest and adjoining western Himalayan region & central India and along the West Coast. Below normal rainfall is likely in many parts of Northeast & adjoining East India and South Peninsular India.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.
texts.text: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  25 September  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 163.34 billion cubic meter, which is 89.5% of total live storage capacity (182.479 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 103.5 percent of last year's storage and 119.3 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  25 September  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 163.34 billion cubic meter, which is 89.5% of total live storage capacity (182.479 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 103.5 percent of last year's storage and 119.3 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: text
texts.orig: the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.
texts.text: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Paddy (Rice Hispa)) in Assam. The Advisory has been issued by  the  survey  team  to  the  farms  and  regular  monitoring  is  being  carried  out.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Paddy (Rice Hispa)) in Assam. The Advisory has been issued by  the  survey  team  to  the  farms  and  regular  monitoring  is  being  carried  out.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · During the week (18 Sept -24 Sept 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 3%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June to 24 th Sept 2025) is higher than LPA by 7%.
texts.text: During the week (18 Sept -24 Sept 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 3%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June to 24 th Sept 2025) is higher than LPA by 7%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be above normal over plains of northwest India, central, east & northeast India; near normal over many parts of south Peninsular India during the week.
texts.text: Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be above normal over plains of northwest India, central, east & northeast India; near normal over many parts of south Peninsular India during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Withdrawal of Southwest Monsoon 2025
texts.text: Withdrawal of Southwest Monsoon 2025

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Southwest  monsoon  has  withdrawal  from  many  parts  of  Gujarat  and  Rajasthan;  entire Punjab and Haryana, Chandigarh & Delhi; some parts of Madhya Pradesh, UP, Himachal Pradesh and Jammu & Kashmir till now.
texts.text: Southwest  monsoon  has  withdrawal  from  many  parts  of  Gujarat  and  Rajasthan;  entire Punjab and Haryana, Chandigarh & Delhi; some parts of Madhya Pradesh, UP, Himachal Pradesh and Jammu & Kashmir till now.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The  line  of  withdrawal  of  southwest  monsoon  continues  to  pass  through,  Veraval, Bharuch, Ujjain, Jhansi and Shahjahanpur.
texts.text: The  line  of  withdrawal  of  southwest  monsoon  continues  to  pass  through,  Veraval, Bharuch, Ujjain, Jhansi and Shahjahanpur.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Conditions are not favourable for further withdrawal of southwest monsoon during next 7 days.
texts.text: Conditions are not favourable for further withdrawal of southwest monsoon during next 7 days.

texts.text: Temerature

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 1 (26 Sept -02 Oct 2025):
texts.text: Week 1 (26 Sept -02 Oct 2025):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, maximum temperatures are likely to be above normal by 3-5°C over most parts of northeast India; above normal by 2-4°C over most parts of northwest India, Gujarat and Bihar. These are very likely to be near normal or below normal over remaining parts of the country during the week.
texts.text: Overall, maximum temperatures are likely to be above normal by 3-5°C over most parts of northeast India; above normal by 2-4°C over most parts of northwest India, Gujarat and Bihar. These are very likely to be near normal or below normal over remaining parts of the country during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (03 Oct -09 Oct 2025):
texts.text: Week 2 (03 Oct -09 Oct 2025):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Maximum temperatures are likely to be near normal or below normal over most parts of the country except Western Himalayan Region, where these are likely to be above normal by 2-4°C during the week.
texts.text: Maximum temperatures are likely to be near normal or below normal over most parts of the country except Western Himalayan Region, where these are likely to be above normal by 2-4°C during the week.

texts.text: 4. REMOTE SENSING BASED SOIL MOISTURE OBSERVATION

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Soil Moisture Observation
texts.text: Soil Moisture Observation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soil moisture as on 19 Sept - 25 Sept 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years  in  major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Gujarat,  Jharkhand, West Bengal, major parts of Odisha, Haryana, Punjab, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Telangana, A.P. and southern parts of Bihar.
texts.text: Soil moisture as on 19 Sept - 25 Sept 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years  in  major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Gujarat,  Jharkhand, West Bengal, major parts of Odisha, Haryana, Punjab, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Telangana, A.P. and southern parts of Bihar.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · It was lower than average of past 9 years in pockets of northern parts of Bihar , in pockets of eastern part of UP, in pockets of western parts of Assam, in pockets of southern parts of Odisha, in pockets of Saurashtra Region of Gujarat and in parts of Tamil Nadu.
texts.text: It was lower than average of past 9 years in pockets of northern parts of Bihar , in pockets of eastern part of UP, in pockets of western parts of Assam, in pockets of southern parts of Odisha, in pockets of Saurashtra Region of Gujarat and in parts of Tamil Nadu.

texts.text: Kharif Crops - Area coverage

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Rice :  About  441.58  lakh  ha  (109.5%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Kharif  Rice  as compared to 435.68 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rice :  About  441.58  lakh  ha  (109.5%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Kharif  Rice  as compared to 435.68 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Maize :  About  94.95  lakh  ha  (120.3%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Maize  has  been reported as compared to 84.30 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Maize :  About  94.95  lakh  ha  (120.3%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Maize  has  been reported as compared to 84.30 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Moong : About 34.84 lakh ha (97.6% of Normal) area coverage under Moong as compared to 34.96 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Moong : About 34.84 lakh ha (97.6% of Normal) area coverage under Moong as compared to 34.96 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.text: Soybean : About 120.45 lakh ha (94.7% of Normal) area coverage under Soyabean has been reported  as  compared  to  129.55  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year. Soyabean area diverted in Maize in MP and Maharashtra.
